title: Mathematics
subtitle: Week 1: [Advanced] Area of Squares and Rectangles using Tiles

slides.title: [Advanced] Area Concept Slide 1
slides.text: Area is the amount of flat space that a shape covers. We can measure area by covering a surface with smaller, identical shapes like square tiles. A square tile has equal sides, making it a perfect unit for measuring area without leaving any gaps or overlaps between the shapes. In the town of Polomolok, farmers layout square wooden crates to estimate the ground space needed for harvesting sweet pineapples near Mount Matutum. How can we use square tiles to measure the flat surface of a table or floor? Let us discuss how this helps us count units. Review detail 1. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By analyze deeplying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems..

examples.title: [Advanced] Example 1
examples.content: Advanced analysis is required: A rectangle has 4 rows and 5 columns of tiles. Total area = 4 multiplied by 5 = 20 square tile units.

slides.title: [Advanced] Area Concept Slide 6
slides.text: To find the area of a rectangle, we can draw grid lines inside it to make rows and columns of square tiles. Counting all the individual squares inside the grid tells us the total area. This visual grid method helps us see how area is made of repeated rows. Students at Koronadal Central Elementary School draw square grids on their paper to plan layout maps of local parks in South Cotabato. If a rectangle is divided into equal rows and columns, how does counting the squares help us find the total area? Study slide 6. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By analyze deeplying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important to investigate.

slides.title: [Advanced] Area Concept Slide 11
slides.text: We can estimate the area of large flat surfaces by placing a few sample square tiles and imagining the rest of the grid. Estimating helps us find a close answer when we do not have enough tiles to cover the entire space, which is very useful in real-world projects. Weavers in Lake Sebu estimate the area of their traditional T'nalak mats by laying down square cardboard templates before starting their weaving process. Why is estimation a useful skill when measuring large areas in our homes and schools? Let us explore this idea in card 11. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By analyze deeplying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important to investigate.

slides.title: [Advanced] Area Concept Slide 16
slides.text: We can derive the area formula by observing that the total number of tiles is equal to the number of rows multiplied by the number of columns. For a rectangle, this is length multiplied by width. For a square, since all sides are equal, it is side multiplied by side. A fruit vendor at the General Santos City public market uses this multiplication rule to calculate the area of their rectangular tuna display table. How does multiplying the number of rows by columns make finding the area faster than counting each tile one by one? Card 16. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By analyze deeplying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important.

slides.title: [Advanced] Area Concept Slide 21
slides.text: Solving area problems is easy once we understand the grid. We can find the missing side if we know the total area and one side length by dividing the area by the known side. Practice using both counting grids and formulas to master these area calculations. Workers building a community center in Tupi use these area formulas to calculate how many square floor tiles they need to order. How can understanding area formulas help builders and decorators save time and materials in their daily work? Summary slide 21. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By analyze deeplying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important to investigate regularly and discuss these ideas with your peers and teachers to build a strong foundation.

challenge.q: [Advanced HOTS] A rectangular pineapple plot in Polomolok has an area of 24 square tile units. If the width of the plot is 4 tile units, what is its length?
[Advanced HOTS] A rectangular pineapple plot in Polomolok has an area of 30 square tile units. If the width of the plot is 5 tile units, what is its length?
[Advanced HOTS] A rectangular pineapple plot in Polomolok has an area of 36 square tile units. If the width of the plot is 6 tile units, what is its length?
[Advanced HOTS] A rectangular pineapple plot in Polomolok has an area of 42 square tile units. If the width of the plot is 7 tile units, what is its length?
[Advanced HOTS] A rectangular pineapple plot in Polomolok has an area of 48 square tile units. If the width of the plot is 8 tile units, what is its length?

pages:
Exercise 1: Counting Tile Areas

Count the number of square tiles in each grid below and write the total area using your stylus.

1. A grid has 3 rows of 6 square tiles each. What is the area?
18 square units
2. A grid has 5 rows of 5 square tiles each. What is the area?
25 square units

Exercise 2: Estimating Box Areas

Estimate the area of each shape based on the given sample tiles. Write your final estimate.

1. A flat pineapple bin in Polomolok can fit 6 columns and 8 rows of fruit boxes. Estimate the total area:
48 boxes

Exercise 3: Area Formula Derivation

Apply the area formulas to calculate the total area in square tile units.

1. A square has a side length of 7 tile units. Area = side multiplied by side. What is its area?
49 square units

performance.title: [Advanced] My Dream Pineapple Farm Plot
performance.desc: Advanced Task: Draw a rectangular grid on a piece of paper representing a pineapple farm plot in Polomolok. Make it 6 units long and 4 units wide. Color each square tile unit yellow for pineapples. Calculate the total area using both counting and the area formula (Area = length multiplied by width). Take a photo of your drawing and upload it here.
